column1: # SUN / SIM Weekly Alignment Dashboard
A static
column2: client-side dashboard that reads the weekly CRM "Leads" export

column1: directly — no reshaping needed. Open `index.html` in a browser
folder on GitHub Pages. All parsing and calculation happens in the visitor's
browser; nothing is ever uploaded anywhere.
## Quick start
1. Open `index.html`.
2. Click **Load sample data** to see it populated with a synthetic dataset
or click **Upload weekly CRM export** and select your actual export file
(the same `.xlsx` you pull from the CRM each week).
3. Switch between entities (SUN / SIM
column2: or whatever accounts are in your

column1: data) with the toggle in the header.
## What file to upload
Upload the CRM's **Leads** export as-is — the same file
you already pull weekly. The dashboard reads it directly. No pivoting
reshaping
**Required columns** (matched case-insensitively
| Column | Used for |
| `Account` | Which entity the lead belongs to (maps "Sunway University - Online" → SUN
column2: "Singapore Institute of Management - Online" → SIM; any other account name is used as its own entity label) |

column1: | `Program` / `Short Name (Program) (Program)` | Program breakdown |
| `Lead Channel` | Channel breakdown (PPC
column4: etc. — whatever appears in your data) |

column1: | `Created On` | When the lead was created — drives the Leads count and its week |
| `Contacted Date` | When first contacted — drives the Contacts count |
| `Interview Completed Date` | Drives the ICs count |
| `Evaluation Completed Date` | Drives the ECs count |
| `File Completed Date` | Drives the Applications / Apps count |
Each upload is treated as a **full refresh** — it replaces whatever was
loaded before
column2: since the export is a rolling extract of all leads to date

column1: not just the new ones. Just upload the latest file each week; you don't
need to merge or trim anything first.
## Weeks run Monday–Sunday
Every date column is bucketed into the Mon–Sun week it actually falls in
labelled by its week-ending Sunday. A lead created in one week that reaches
Interview Complete two weeks later shows up correctly in both weeks'
figures — leads counted the week they arrived
counted the week each milestone happened.
**"Latest week" always means the last fully-completed Mon–Sun week.** If
you upload the file on a Monday and it already contains a few leads created
that same morning
column2: those get bucketed into the new (still in-progress)

column1: week and are excluded from the headline KPI cards and the funnel panel —
so Monday's view always reflects the week that just ended
sliver of the new one. They still show up in the trend charts
naturally lower
## What's on the dashboard
- **KPI row** — last full week's Leads
Rate
column2: and IC→EC Rate (flagged "watch" — historically the bottleneck

column1: stage)
- **Leads by Channel** — line chart
column2: one line per channel that appears in

column1: your data.
- **Leads & Applications by Program** — rolled-up totals on a dual axis.
- **Funnel Stage Rates** — C→IC
- **Funnel (latest week)** — a waterfall-style bar view of
Contacts → ICs → ECs → Apps for the last completed week.
- **Program Detail table** — last completed week
## A known gap: no advisor-level detail or Handles
This export is a lead-lifecycle file — it has no advisor/owner column and
no call-activity data
column2: so there's nothing in it to build a per-advisor

column1: breakdown or a "Handles" (dial volume) metric from. Everything on this
dashboard is entity-level
column2: built purely from lead and milestone dates.

column1: If you get a separate call-activity/dialer export later (one with an
advisor column and call counts)
column2: that can be wired in as an additional

column1: upload and advisor-level detail can be added back — just share a sample
of that export's columns and it can be built the same way this one was.
## Deploying to GitHub Pages
1. Put these files together in one repo folder: `index.html`
`app.js`
2. Push to GitHub.
3. **Settings → Pages** → set Source to your branch
4. GitHub gives you a live URL — that's the dashboard.
If this dashboard shares a repo with anything else
subfolder or its own repo entirely — GitHub Pages serves whatever
`index.html` it finds
column2: so a filename collision with another project will

column1: silently overwrite one or the other.
## Data persistence
Once loaded
column2: data is cached in the browser's `localStorage` so it's still

column1: there if you close and reopen the page
column2: until you upload a new file or

column1: click **Clear loaded data**. This is per-browser
sync anywhere or get shared with anyone else who opens the page.
## Customising
- **Colours**: edit the CSS custom properties at the top of `style.css`
- **Programs/channels**: no code change needed — whatever values appear in
your data are picked up automatically.
- **Column mapping**: if the CRM export's column names ever change
the lowercase key lookups in `transformCRMRows()` in `app.js`.
## Fully offline / no external network dependencies
The three JS libraries this dashboard uses (Chart.js
are bundled locally in the `vendor/` folder rather than loaded from a CDN.
This matters if you're on a network that blocks common CDN domains
(`cdnjs.cloudflare.com`
column3: etc.) — a real issue we hit while

request the page makes is for the Google Fonts stylesheet (cosmetic only —
if that's blocked too
column2: it just falls back to a system font
